key_themes: Strong Q4/FY26 earnings beat with robust comp sales (+5%) and margin expansion well above plan
Shareholder returns: 13% dividend increase and $2.5–2.75B share repurchase authorization for FY27
Resilient off-price/value retail model benefiting from consumer trade-down in uncertain economy
High ROIC, consistent growth, and quality defensive characteristics
Broad analyst Buy ratings with multiple price target increases post-earnings
summary: TJX reported excellent Q4 and full-year FY26 results in late February, with comparable sales, pretax margins, and EPS significantly beating internal plans and consensus. Management responded with a 13% dividend raise in March and a large buyback program while guiding FY27 modestly below some Street estimates amid softer consumer spending signals. Investors and analysts remain bullish on the company's proven off-price model, steady margins, high returns on capital, and ability to thrive on value-seeking behavior, supporting steady stock performance near all-time highs around $160–165.
sources: 10+ recent news/articles from TJX investor site, Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat, TipRanks, Reuters (earnings, dividend, analyst notes), ~20 recent X posts (mostly bullish investor commentary; technicals, consistency, portfolio inclusions), Company press releases (Feb/Mar 2026) and stock performance data through mid-April 2026
